titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the John Holmstrom Papers and Punk Magazine Records
Holmstrom (John) papers and Punk Magazine Records
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday September 3, 2018 at 23:51

did.unittitle: John Holmstrom papers and Punk magazine records

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, writings, photographs, maquettes, printed materials, posters, ephemera, audiovisual materials, artwork, and other records relating to Punk Magazine and John Holmstrom.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: Series I. Artwork. Series II. Writings and Correspondence. Series III. Business Records. IV. Photographs. Series V. Printed Materials. Series VI. Merchandise. Series VII. Ephemera and Realia. Series VIII. Audiovisual Materials.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Johan Kugelberg on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and gift of John Holmstrom, 2012.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: John Holmstrom Papers and Punk Magazine Archive. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

did.unittitle: Art for Punk #0 Supplement in High Times February 1977
Punk issue #15 (Mutant Monster Beach Party), hand-drawn maquette

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Unpublished Punk Issue #18, Art for Punk Clanadar 1979
Art from Punk Special Edition: D.O.A.: The Official Filmbook
Art from Punk Special Edition: D.O.A.: The Official Filmbook
Punk V.2 #0 1996 book, Punk #19, #20; miscellaneous Punk Magazine-related artwork
Punk Issues #6 (from A-13), #11 (from A-7), #14 (from A-13), #14 (from Framed), #16 (from Framed).
Legend of Nick Detroit film project. DELETE ME?!
Contains at material from supplement 1 and 2
Printed proofs and pay copies of Punk and other publications
Issues of The Spark and other publications
Mug with wool hat, stickers, key chains, four baseball hats, buttons, two energy drink cans
Punk bag, two purses, belts, ruler, T-shirt, baseball hat
John Holmstrom ephemera, fliers, news clippings, and ephemera
Ticket stubs, movie, band, and album press kits, stickers, flyers, posters

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Artifacts from CBGB's: Flier-covered section of wall from CBGB's
Cowboy boots worn by John Holmstrom on the 1978 Sex Pistols tour.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A note from John Holmstrom is on the sole of each boot.

did.materialspec: The timecode runs through the whole video.

did.unittitle: Iggy Pop, Live in Paris A L' Olympia, Part I of II, running time 56:59
F# Sharp TV, The Pink Show and John Holmstrom
Joey Ramones 50th Birthday Party at Hammerstein Ballroom, NYC, 38:00 raw footage (tape 2)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: List and description of the cartoons are included with the tape.

list.item: 1. Warner Bros. "Sunday Go to Meetin' Time" (negro skips church and pays the price)
2. Warner Bros. "Confederate Honey" (early Elmer Fudd joins the Confederate army)
3. Warner Bros. "All This & Rabbit Stew" (Bugs with black sterotype [?] of Elmer
4. Warner Bros. "Bugs Bunny Nips the Nips" (classic anti-Japanese wartime)
5. Warner Bros. "Russian Rhapsody" (Hitler and "Gremlins from the Kremlin") - B. Clampett
6. Warner Bros. "Angel Puss" (black child "Sambo" and white cat) - Chuck Jones
7. Warner Bros. "Goldilocks and the Jivin' Bears" (three black bears do the jive and the wolf dances with Goldilocks)
8. Warner Bros. "Scrap Happy Daffy" (black and white, anti-Nazi Daffy Duck becomes super American) - Cashlin
9. Warner Bros. "The Ducktators" (war cartoon with Mussolini and Hitler and Chamberlain characters) - Norm McCabe
10. Warner Bros. Bugs Bunny defense bonds promo (Bugs does black-face bit - Al Jolson)
11. Warner Bros. "Coal Black and the Sebben Dwarves" (classic wartime cartoon) - Clampett
12. Porky Pig - "Son of a Bitch"
13. "Scrub Me Mama with a Boogie Beat" - Walter Lantz; jive-music cartoon "Lazy Town"
14. "John Henry" puppettoon - George Pal; [?] folktale/fable
15. "Jasper on a [Vana?]" puppettoon - George Pal; Peggy Lee vocal
16. Amos & Andy in "Rassling Match"
17. Amos & Andy on "The Lion Tamer"
18. Warner Bros. "The Early Worm Gets the Bird"; incomplete, ends after a couple of minutes (crow birds)

did.unittitle: Joey Ramones 50th Birthday Party at Hammerstein Ballroom, NYC, 1:68:00 raw footage (tape 1)
Warner Reprise Music Video, Sex Pistols, "The Great Rock 'n Roll Swindle"
Dee Dee Ramone Tribute at the Continental, F#TV raw footage

did.materialspec: After the segment on Iggy Pop, a poor quality Ramones video follows. After that, other better-quality Ramones video appears including a biographical video.

did.unittitle: Punk Mag Show at CBGB's, raw footage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A typed letter included with the tape reads: Hi John, Here is a tape with all the footage I got the night of the Punk Magazine show at CBGB's and the gallery. We had a blast playing and just being there was an experience. A few weeks after I saw the horn player that was in the band doing "My Generation". It was a PBS tribute to Doo-Wop and he was in the horn section. I can only hope to see Thor and Handsome Dick on the Food Network or Home Shopping Channel. Regards, Tommy White

did.unittitle: Iggy Pop, Live in Paris A L' Olympia, Part II of II, running time 52:44
did.materialspec: The tape has noise in the audio from time to time. The video is fair. There is a timecode that runs through the tape.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Iggy in Paris - Kiss My Blood" DVD recorded live @ the Olympia in Paris: 1991-03-15. Included with the tape is a list of tracks and song writers listed below.

list.item: 1. "Raw Power" - Iggy Pop, James Williamson
2."Five Foot One" - Iggy Pop
3. "Dirt" - Stooges AKA James Osterberg (Iggy Pop), Dave Alexander, Ron Asheton, Scott Asheton
4. "Loose" - Stooges AKA James Osterberg (Iggy Pop), Dave Alexander, Ron Asheton, Scott Asheton
5. "Lust for Life" - Iggy Pop, David Bowie
6. "China Girl" - Iggy Pop, David Bowie
7. "I Got a Right" - Iggy Pop
8. "Butt Town" - Iggy Pop
9. "Real Wild Child" - Johnny O'Keefe, Johnny Greenan, Dave Owens
10. My Baby Wants to Rock and Roll - Iggy Pop, Slash
11. "Neon Forest" - Iggy Pop
12. "Home" - Iggy Pop
13. "Brick by Brick" - Iggy Pop
14. "1969" - Stooges AKA James Osterberg (Iggy Pop), Dave Alexander, Ron Asheton, Scott Asheton
15. "Candy" - Iggy Pop
16. "I Wanna Be Your Dog" - Stooges AKA James Osterberg (Iggy Pop), Dave Alexander, Ron Asheton, Scott Asheton
17. "No Fun" - Stooges AKA James Osterberg (Iggy Pop), Dave Alexander, Ron Asheton, Scott Asheton
18. "Search and Destroy" - Iggy Pop, James Williamson
19. "Down on the Street" - Stooges AKA James Osterberg (Iggy Pop), Dave Alexander, Ron Asheton, Scott Asheton
20. "The Passenger" - Iggy Pop, Ricky Gardner
21. "Louie Louie" - Richard Berry
22. "Foxy Lady" - Jimi Hendrix

did.unittitle: Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
Jerry Springer "Holiday Hell: Feast IV"; Blondes (Blondie segment), Bizarre, episode 3000
did.materialspec: This tape has not been digitized. There was no reference of Blondie found on this tape.

did.unittitle: Iggy and The Stooges-Coachella, 1970-Cincinatti/Goose Lake/Dinah etc.
The Stooges, Wantagh, NY at Jones Beach Theatre, 2003 August 8, 66 minutes; Iggy Pop, "La Rage De Virve-Arte TV" (SPAIN TV) 1997, 51 minutes. Lots of cool Iggy clips from the 1970s.

did.materialspec: A timecode runs through the entire tape.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
